test_suite.details: All 2989 tests pass. Schema, HTML consistency, internal links, build pipeline, data cross-references, sections.json schema, and prose content integrity all verified.

internal_anchors.details: All href='#...' targets and showTab() calls verified by test suite. Not re-checked manually per prompt instructions.

tab_structure.nav_chain: overview → evaluate → model → selftest → wins → pages → killshots → predictions → falsify → ai → refs
tab_structure.heading_hierarchy_note: h1→h3 skips detected in model, selftest, wins, pages, killshots, predictions, falsify, ai tabs. This is a design pattern (tabs use h1 as section header, h3 for individual WIN/topic headers with no h2 tier). Not a structural defect.
tab_structure.details: 10 unique tabs, 11 section-nav blocks forming continuous chain, no duplicates, no orphans. overview is default active tab.

bot_blocked.note: Returns 403 even with browser user-agent. Likely Cloudflare bot protection. Site is accessible via browser. Not a broken link.

external_links.details: Spot-checked 10 key data source URLs. 9/10 returned 200 OK. NOAA.gov returns 403 (bot protection, not broken). Dome site, INTERMAGNET, NASA, ESA, NCEI, NOAA Solar Calculator, CelesTrak, GPS.gov, CymaScope all reachable.

data_prose_consistency.details: Build ran successfully and produced HTML matching GitHub. wins.json has 70 entries. All verdict tallies computed: Refuted by Data=9, Self-Contradicted=13, Misleading=23, Std Model Explains=17, Not Demonstrated=4, Unfalsifiable=4. 31 new_in_v51 WINs. Summary table row count verified via build — all counts are computed at build time from wins.json, no hardcoded values.

win_detail_consistency.details: All 70 WIN IDs in wins.json have corresponding anchors (id='winNNN') in the HTML. All detail groups internally consistent. All new_in_v51 WINs present.

win_alignment_dome_site.title_drift: Minor wording differences but no semantic drift. Our claims track the dome's claims correctly.
win_alignment_dome_site.details: Dome site lists WIN-001 through WIN-069 plus PROS-001 through PROS-008. We have 70 WINs including WIN-058b (our split). WIN-010 and WIN-025 are marked removed on dome site but we still review them (correct — we review what was claimed). PROS aliases inflate the dome's count. No number collisions or redefinitons detected.

discoverability.details: All discoverability files present and well-formed. llms.txt references correct WIN count (70). Meta tags and ClaimReview structured data present.

expansion_tracker.priority_queue_coupling: FAIL: EXP-050 (category-proposal-writeup) missing from priority queue
expansion_tracker.details: next_id=70 > max_id=69: OK. EXP-052 gap (single). Two phantom entries have N/A output files (direct decider integration, not real phantoms). EXP-050 is uncoupled from priority queue — major finding.

workspace_only_files.details: 2 curmudgeon reviews and 11 analyst expansions exist on workspace but not in git. These are at risk of silent data loss if the workspace is recycled. The workspace-sync agent should commit these.

workspace_sync_skip_log.details: MAJOR: Two files have repeated direction-violation skips in 24h. CAT-005.json (4 skips) suggests a writer is editing a git-owned file from the workspace side. review-state.json (3 skips) is workspace-owned per CLAUDE.md — this may be a misclassification in the workspace-sync direction guard.

project_documentation.details: All 10 spot-checked file paths exist. No hardcoded counts found. However, 16 reference files in monitor/prompts/reference/ and data/kill-shots.json are not listed in CLAUDE.md's file map. These are likely newer additions after a V6 restructure that split prompt files.

build_reproducibility.details: Freshly built HTML from data/wins.json + data/sections.json matches the published docs/index.html on GitHub exactly (0 diff lines). No build drift.

issues_found.description: EXP-050 (category-proposal-writeup) has no matching entry in curmudgeon priority-queue.json. Prose will land without adversarial review.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Decider should push EXP-050 onto priority-queue.json with target_id='EXP-050'.

issues_found.description: 13 files exist on workspace FUSE mount but not in git: 2 curmudgeon reviews (PRED-batch-2026-04-11.json, part6-6.5-rewrite.json) and 11 analyst expansions (ISS-715-finding, 7 PRED-CURR assessments, 3 PRED-V13 assessments). At risk of silent data loss.
issues_found.suggested_fix: workspace-sync agent should commit these files to git on next run.

issues_found.description: Repeated workspace-sync direction-violation skips: CAT-005.json (4 skips in 24h) and review-state.json (3 skips). CAT-005.json appears to be written from the wrong side of the boundary. review-state.json is workspace-owned — check if the direction guard has it misclassified.
issues_found.suggested_fix: For CAT-005.json: trace which agent writes it and ensure it writes to git clone, not workspace. For review-state.json: verify it is classified as workspace-owned in the direction guard; if so, the guard should not be skipping it.

issues_found.description: 16 reference files in monitor/prompts/reference/ and data/kill-shots.json are not listed in CLAUDE.md. These were likely added during V6 restructure (prompt splitting) but the file map was not updated.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Add the 16 new reference .md files and kill-shots.json to CLAUDE.md's file map and agent-specific reference table.

issues_found.description: EXP-052 ID gap in expansion tracker. Two phantom entries (EXP-058, EXP-061) have N/A output files — these are direct decider integrations, not real errors, but the tracker schema should accommodate this cleanly.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Investigate whether EXP-052 was deleted or never created. Consider adding a status like 'direct_integration' for items that bypass the normal output file pipeline.

issues_found.description: https://www.noaa.gov/ returns HTTP 403 (Cloudflare bot protection). Site is accessible via browser. Not a real broken link.
issues_found.suggested_fix: No action needed. This is a known bot-blocking pattern. The NCEI subdomain (ncei.noaa.gov) which hosts the actual data pages returns 200 OK.

summary: Site is structurally sound. Test suite passes (2989/2989), build output matches GitHub (no drift), all 70 WIN anchors present, tab navigation chain intact, discoverability infrastructure complete. Three major pipeline issues need attention: (1) EXP-050 is uncoupled from the curmudgeon priority queue, risking unreviewed prose integration; (2) 13 files exist only on the FUSE workspace and are at risk of data loss; (3) repeated direction-violation skips in workspace-sync for CAT-005.json and review-state.json indicate a boundary-crossing writer or guard misclassification. CLAUDE.md file map is missing 17 files added during V6 restructure. No build drift, no broken internal links, no data-prose mismatches.
